info.title: What is madhhab
info.paragraph1: Madhhab is an Arabic term that means a school of Islamic law. Four madhhabs are the most common among Sunni Muslims: Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki and Hanbali.
info.paragraph2: The word madhhab comes from the Arabic root “zahaba”, which means “to go” or “to follow a path”.

hanafi.title: Hanafi madhhab
hanafi.body: The most widespread madhhab in many regions. It is known for a systematic fiqh approach and broad commentary tradition.

maliki.title: Maliki madhhab
maliki.body: Built in part on the practice of the people of Medina as an important source. Widespread in North and West Africa.

shafii.title: Shafi'i madhhab
shafii.body: Places strong emphasis on hadith and usul al-fiqh methodology. Widespread in parts of the Middle East and Asia.

hanbali.title: Hanbali madhhab
hanbali.body: Known for strict reliance on primary textual sources and evidence. Widespread in some Arab countries.

about.title: About the project
about.paragraph1: Prayer is the most important precept of religion.
about.paragraph2: This guide helps you learn step by step.

support.description: Your subscription helps us improve the app and ship new updates.
support.disclaimer: Subscription renews automatically unless cancelled in advance.

takbir.title: Takbir
takbir.description: Standing, raise your hands and say:

istiadha.title: Seeking protection
istiadha.description: Place your hands on your chest and say:
istiadha.translation: I seek Allah's protection from the cursed devil

ablution.title: Ablution (Wudu)
ablution.description: Step-by-step guide to performing ablution before prayer

1.title: Intention
1.description: Before wudu, make intention in your heart, then say:

2.title: Washing your wrists
2.description: Wash both hands including the wrists and between the fingers (3 times)

3.title: Mouth rinsing
3.description: Rinse the mouth using the right hand (3 times)

4.title: Nose flushing
4.description: Sniff water into the nostrils and expel it (3 times)

5.title: Face washing
5.description: Wash the entire face from forehead to chin and ear to ear (3 times)

6.title: Hand washing
6.description: Wash both arms up to and including the elbows, starting with the right (3 times each)

7.title: Head wiping
7.description: Wipe the head with wet hands from front to back and back again (once)

8.title: Wiping the ears
8.description: Wipe the inside and back of both ears (once)

9.title: Foot washing
9.description: Wash both feet including the ankles and between the toes, starting with the right (3 times each)

al_fatiha.1: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
al_fatiha.2: All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds
al_fatiha.3: The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
al_fatiha.4: Master of the Day of Judgment
al_fatiha.5: You alone we worship and You alone we ask for help
al_fatiha.6: Guide us to the straight path
al_fatiha.7: The path of those You have blessed

al_ikhlas.0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
al_ikhlas.1: Say: He is Allah, the One
al_ikhlas.3: He neither begets nor is born
al_ikhlas.4: Nor is there to Him any equivalent

al_falaq.0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
al_falaq.1: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak
al_falaq.2: From the evil of what He created
al_falaq.3: From the evil of darkness when it settles
al_falaq.4: From the evil of those who blow on knots
al_falaq.5: From the evil of an envier when he envies

an_nas.0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
an_nas.1: Say: I seek refuge with the Lord of Mankind
an_nas.4: From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws (when one remembers Allah)
an_nas.5: Who whispers in the breasts of mankind
an_nas.6: From among the jinn and mankind

at_tahiyat.1: All compliments, prayers and pure words are due to Allah
at_tahiyat.2: Peace be upon you, O Prophet, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings
at_tahiyat.3: Peace be upon us and on the righteous slaves of Allah
at_tahiyat.4: I bear witness that there is no deity worthy of worship except Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His slave and Messenger

salawat.1: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad
salawat.2: As You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim
salawat.3: Indeed You are Praiseworthy, Most Glorious
salawat.4: And send Your blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad
salawat.5: As You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim
salawat.6: Indeed You are Praiseworthy, Most Glorious

s1.title: Takbir
s1.description: Standing, he directs his gaze to the spot where his forehead will touch during prostration, raises his hands to the level of his ears or shoulders and says:

s2.title: A prayer for protection from the devil
s2.description: He places his hands on his chest, laying his right hand over his left, and says:
s2.text: I seek Allah's protection from the cursed devil

s5.title: Reading additional Surahs
s5.description: Reciting additional Surahs is not obligatory. Any of the listed Surahs can be recited after Al-Fatiha.

s7.title: Waist bow (ruku)
s7.description: You should now be in the bowing (rukoo') position. Say this 3 times:
s7.text: Glory be to my Lord the Supreme

s8.title: Straightening
s8.description: Next, come up from the bowing position into the standing position. As you are coming up say:
s8.text: Allah listens to the one who praises him

s9.title: Standing
s9.description: Now you should be in the standing position with your hands by your sides. In this position say:
s9.text: Our Lord, and to You belongs the praise
